title: Native modules and the Electron ABI
description: Native addons are compiled against a specific Node/V8 ABI; Electron ships its own, so modules built for system Node may fail to load.
body: Use when a native addon crashes or refuses to load. Native modules link against a particular Node/V8 ABI version; Electron bundles its own Node build, so a module installed/compiled for the system Node can be ABI-incompatible and error on load. N-API modules are ABI-stable and avoid this; others need rebuilding for the app's Electron version. Do not assume an npm-installed native module just works in Electron. Actions: use N-API/prebuilt addons or electron-rebuild per target. Validation signals include the module loading on every shipped platform/arch. Risk boundary: an ABI mismatch can crash the process at startup on a user's machine even if it worked in dev — rebuild and test natives per platform before shipping.
